mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median 60-day returns or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable consumer prices confirms healthy capacity utilization without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations through improved profit margins.
failure_condition: Mechanism fails when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized, causing profit margin compression and negative forward returns despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive short-term market momentum.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC remains negative or 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 2.0 percent signals a restrictive monetary stance that increases borrowing costs for corporations and households, suppressing investment and generating negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of instances following the threshold crossing, invalidating the restrictive rate penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak household demand relative to overall output, creating inventory overhangs that pressure corporate earnings and equity prices.
failure_condition: Interaction effect reverses sign or loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when consumer confidence indices remain elevated despite the divergence.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a high unemployment environment signals a deepening economic contraction that triggers immediate flight-to-safety flows and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 8.0% and INDPRO declines.
